item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A fascinating mystery. I don't get scared easily by fiction, but this came close. The idea of doppelgangers, completely indistinguishable from real people, spawned into the world by some horrible, cruel process… Beautiful.

That said, tying it so directly to the real-life case drags it down significantly. If you wanted to involve Bobby Dunbar, you should have been more subtle about it and, at least, merely implied it. As it stands, this is most distracting.

+1, nevertheless. Horror aspect's too good.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This image originates from a now-deleted DeviantArt account back in 2011. As such, there is no indication that the image was released under CC 3.0 and is assumed to be under DeviantArt's license still, which is all rights reserved and thus not compliant with the wiki. The image has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it but I didn't get it. I feel there's something to get but I just can't see it so the scip loses some value to me. It's nice, though, so you get an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see the horror, I see the implications, I see the possibilities. I see that its lacking something. It's a good one-two combo, but I'm still waiting for the straight right to hit me in the liver. I'm sorry I'm not being specific.

No vote. I will revisit this in the future, and see if that holds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the sheer amount of WTF factor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I just adore this article! Things beyond understanding happening, strange Changeling-like beings emerging from a sinkhole in a swamp, that picture!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very old school vibe, +1.

Although you never actually explain what 1692-3 instances are which left me confused for a second. It's pretty easy to figure out but I think it's in keeping with foundation articles to at least say "SCP 1692-3 refers to …" blah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with this. In fact…redacting my upvote until it's changed/removed.

Goodwill is a stinky diaper and didn't think to look at the article again before posting this comment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I love SCPs that have that simple, elegant, creepypasta-style flair to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Between several hours to several weeks

Only use "between" with "and". I changed it this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DNA from the body in Foundation custody was found to have been severely mutated via hydrolytic deamination and acute concentrations of radiation not naturally occurring.

The clause here about abnormal levels of radiation is awkwardly worded. Please revise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was raised on Series 1.

Also, I removed that part from containment, since it doesn't make sense like you say

item.title: Refreshing
item.content:encoded:
Ah. No redundant interview logs or self serving universe building, and enough is left out to let me think.

Love the southeast creepy swamp vibes, and the dreadful image of a something haunting a sinkhole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I sometimes wonder if this site isn't moving too far away from its creepypasta roots, and then something like this comes along. Nice atmospheric work.

One quibble: The containment procedures seem to set up that -3s emerge after someone's been pulled into the sinkhole. But from the description, it sounds they just emerge after irregular intervals. Feels like an inconsistency to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture of Allison Harvard is waaay too immediately recognizable. I think she was even on one of those 'Next Top Model' shows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hrm… I'm not sure I like that first picture. For one thing, I immediately recognized the source (It was a few years back, but it was part of a meme for a while). From a more objective point of view - that is a really weird place to put a security camera perspective wise.

Interviews with the now-adult Bobby Dunbar provided no pertinent information regarding the body, though had mentioned in an earlier interview a memory of "the other boy on the wagon" but could provide no further details.

This bit here reads a little bit awkwardly to me.

I'm not sure I like the Bobby Dunbar bit - but I still like the overall execution enough to upvote.

Nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the classic, good creepy feeling everyone loves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I stand corrected. Anyway, just a small little thing, I wouldn't worry about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's St. Landry Parish 0_o unless my geography/history is horribly off, but that's what Wikipedia says.

You're right, though, any attempt of secrecy is lost when you realize who the people described are

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nicely done. Good imagery, and the discovery of the anomaly predating the documentation by such a long period gives this a nice archival feel that I find pleasing.

Couple of nitpicks: It's obviously La Salle Parish; you may want to change the redaction on that. And in one of the sentences you have, it sounds like a guy has a woman named Charles working for him.

Overall though, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although part of me is going "No, no you can't" the second you mention Bobby Dunbar, the rest of it is so classy and creepy southern gothic horror that I can't help but +1 it.

But I just get a little heartbroken by the whole Bobby Dunbar thing, so I'm torn, y'know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh… yes, I like this. If there's anything like it on the site, I haven't read it yet. Leaves just enough to the imagination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd change "white officer" to "Caucasian", but otherwise good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't know what to think of this one, really. Pretty creepy and well written with a good backstory.

Ah who am I kidding? Might not be exactly my cup o tea, but it's still good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1, I like how this leaves so much to the imagination. I'm not sure whats going on, but it's my favorite flavor of creepy.
I'm curious - what would show up if they tried GPR (or whatever) imaging?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A kelpie? A classical changeling? Very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of Event Horizon, in a good way (because that movie was great). The Bobby Dunbar thing just put it over the top and earned my upvote.

item.title: It reminds me of Mushishi in a good way.
item.content:encoded: Ilikeit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting creepy concept. The sinkhole in the swamp is such an eerie image in my mind. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no, it's a sarlacc!

All kidding aside, nice work! Very creepy and twisty.
